resources.string: System prompt (optional) — shapes the agent\'s personality and behaviour

This wizard will help you set up the SDG Claw Termux bridge — a small Node.js server that runs inside Termux and lets the AI agent execute commands on your device.

You will need:
• Termux (from F-Droid)
• An internet connection for downloading packages

The setup takes about 5 minutes.

Termux is a terminal emulator and Linux environment for Android.

1. Open F-Droid on your device (install it from f-droid.org if needed).
2. Search for "Termux" and install the official package.

Do NOT install Termux from the Google Play Store — that version is outdated and will cause errors.

Once installed, open Termux and let it finish its initial setup, then tap Next.

Inside Termux, run the commands below to update packages and install Node.js (≥ 16).\n\nCopy each line and paste it into the Termux terminal, then press Enter and wait for it to finish before moving to the next line.

Tap the button below to copy the SDG Claw bridge setup script (setup.sh) from this app to your device\'s shared storage.\n\nAfter copying, the file path will be shown here and copied to your clipboard automatically so you can paste it into Termux.

setup.sh has been saved to:

%1$s

The path has been copied to your clipboard. In Termux, run:
 bash <paste path here>

Then tap Next.

In Termux, run the setup script you copied in the previous step.

Replace <path> with the path shown on the previous screen (it's in your clipboard).

The script will:
 • Create ~/sdgclaw-bridge/
 • Write server.js
 • Install the ws npm package
 • Start the bridge server automatically

Leave Termux open and running in the background, then tap Next.
Make sure the bridge server is running in Termux (you should see "Listening on ws://127.0.0.1:8765" in the terminal).\n\nTap "Verify Connection" below to test that SDG Claw can reach the bridge.
✗ Could not connect. Make sure the bridge is running in Termux and try again.
setup.sh already exists at %1$s. Tap again to overwrite.
